name: Flower (Federated Learning Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Flower Labs / Beutel, Topal, Sherborne

auth.notes: gRPC transport; optional TLS certificates for secure federation

performance.notes: Network-bound; training time is sum of client training + aggregation rounds

agent_readiness.notes: Framework-agnostic federated learning. import flwr as fl. class FlowerClient(fl.client.NumPyClient): def get_parameters(self, config): return model.get_weights(). def fit(self, params, config): model.set_weights(params); model.fit(X, y); return model.get_weights(), len(X), {}. def evaluate(self, params, config): model.set_weights(params); loss, acc = model.evaluate(X_test, y_test); return loss, len(X_test), {'accuracy': acc}. fl.client.start_numpy_client('localhost:8080', FlowerClient()). Server: fl.server.start_server(config=fl.server.ServerConfig(num_rounds=3)). Strategies: FedAvg, FedProx, FedAdam, FedYogi. Simulation: fl.simulation.start_simulation. Framework: TF, PyTorch, sklearn, any.

gotchas: gRPC server/client architecture — must manage server separately
Federated learning requires careful data distribution design (IID vs non-IID)
Communication overhead dominates for large models
Stragglers and client dropouts must be handled with strategies
Privacy not built-in — combine with Opacus for DP-FL
best_when: Federated learning across distributed clients (mobile, IoT, hospitals)
Privacy-preserving ML without centralizing sensitive data
Framework-agnostic FL (works with TF, PyTorch, sklearn, JAX)
Research FL simulations with many clients
avoid_when: When differentially private FL is needed (combine with Opacus)
Production deployment at scale (use PySyft or enterprise FL platforms)
summary: Flower (flwr) provides framework-agnostic federated learning. FlowerClient(NumPyClient): get_parameters(), fit(), evaluate(). start_numpy_client('server:8080'). Server: start_server(num_rounds=3). FedAvg, FedProx, FedAdam strategies. Simulation mode. Works with TF, PyTorch, sklearn. Apache-2.0 by Flower Labs. Best for cross-device federated training. DP requires adding Opacus. gRPC communication overhead for large models. Non-IID data distribution handling needed.
